questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The highest common factor (HCF) of $24$ and $36$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** HCF is the product of common prime factors at lowest powers.

**Working.** $24=2^3\cdot 3$, $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$; HCF $=2^2\cdot 3=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The least common multiple (LCM) of $6$ and $8$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** LCM is the product of all prime factors at highest powers.

**Working.** $6=2\cdot 3$, $8=2^3$; LCM $=2^3\cdot 3=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The probability of getting a head in a single toss of a fair coin is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probability = favourable / total equally likely outcomes.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{2}=0.5$.

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A company's headcount by department is A $30$, B $50$, C $20$ (see bar chart). Department B's share of the total workforce is _____ % .

figure.caption: Bar chart of department headcounts: A 30, B 50, C 20.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='200' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='50' y='100' width='30' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='100' y='60' width='30' height='100' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='150' y='120' width='30' height='40' fill='#f59e0b'/><text x='60' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>A</text><text x='110' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>B</text><text x='160' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>C</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share $=\dfrac{\text{part}}{\text{total}}\times100$.

**Working.** Total $=30+50+20=100$; B's share $=\dfrac{50}{100}\times100 = 50\%$.

**Answer.** $50\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle has one line drawn from its apex to the midpoint of the base (shown). How many triangles are there in total?

figure.caption: A triangle with a single cevian from the apex to the midpoint of the base.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='80,20 20,110 140,110' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='110' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count the small triangles and the whole.

**Working.** The cevian makes $2$ small triangles, plus the original large triangle $=2+1=3$.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: SEISMOGRAPH : EARTHQUAKE :: ANEMOMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : phenomenon it records/measures'.

**Working.** A seismograph records earthquakes; an anemometer measures wind speed.

**Answer.** wind speed.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A bag has $4$ white and $6$ black balls. Two balls are drawn without replacement. The probability that both are white is _____ (round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply conditional probabilities for draws without replacement.

**Working.** $\dfrac{4}{10}\times\dfrac{3}{9}=\dfrac{12}{90}=\dfrac{2}{15}\approx 0.13$.

**Answer.** $\approx 0.13$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points upward (north). After rotating it $180^\circ$, it points

figure.caption: A vertical arrow pointing upward.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='40' y2='30' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='40,30 32,45 48,45' fill='#10b981'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $180^\circ$ rotation reverses the direction.

**Working.** Upward becomes downward.

**Answer.** Downward (south).

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management

**Concept.** Life = air-space/annual fill, both read from the figure.

**Working.**

**Answer.** $6\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Which device is most effective for removing very fine particulates (< 1 µm) at high efficiency?

questions.solution:
**Concept.** ESPs and fabric filters capture sub-micron particles; settling chambers/cyclones are for coarse particles.

**Working.**
ESP.

**Answer.** Electrostatic precipitator

questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $4$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$10000\times0.1^{3}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10$

questions.stem: $4$ identical sources each $80\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log4=86.02.$$

**Answer.** $86.02\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Which stage of the EIA cycle immediately follows screening?

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="8" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="38" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Screening</text><path d="M68 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="78" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="108" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Scoping</text><path d="M138 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="148" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="178" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Impact</text><text x="178" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">prediction</text><path d="M208 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="218" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="248" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">EMP</text><path d="M278 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="288" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="318" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Review &</text><text x="318" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">clearance</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order: screening → scoping → impact prediction → EMP → review/clearance.

**Working.**
Scoping follows screening.

**Answer.** Scoping

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Which characteristics define a hazardous waste under typical regulatory frameworks?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The four hazard characteristics are ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity and toxicity; moisture content is not one.

**Working.**
Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity.

**Answer.** Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: An exporter's overseas buyers demand third-party certification of an audited, continually-improving system that reduces the environmental impact of its operations — not product quality, not worker safety, not information security. The exporter should certify to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match the scope to the standard: ISO 14001 = Environmental Management System (PDCA over environmental aspects); ISO 9001 = quality; ISO 45001 = occupational health & safety; ISO 27001 = information security.

**Working.**
Scope is environmental impact ⇒ ISO 14001.

**Answer.** ISO 14001

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 8000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each transfer retains ~10%: $E_n=E_0(0.1)^n$, with $E_0$ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$8000\times0.1^{2}=80.$$

**Answer.** $80\,\text{kcal/m}^2/\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">8</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$8+1.5=9.5.$$

**Answer.** $9.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $10$ identical sources each $80\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log10=90.$$

**Answer.** $90\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $3$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 12000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>
figure.caption: Only the producer (trophic level 1) energy is marked; upper levels follow the 10% rule. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$12000\times0.1^{2}=120.$$

**Answer.** $120$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: For the landfill shown, read the usable air-space and the annual fill rate from the diagram. Its design life is _____ years.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="100" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.45"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><text x="170" y="86" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">air-space = 5,00,000 m³</text><text x="170" y="108" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">fill rate = 40,000 m³/yr</text><text x="170" y="166" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">design life = air-space ÷ fill rate</text></svg>
figure.caption: Landfill air-space and annual fill rate marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life = air-space/annual fill, both read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{500000}{40000}=12.5.$$

**Answer.** $12.5\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A treatment plant receives BOD $250\,\text{mg/L}$ and discharges $25\,\text{mg/L}$. The BOD removal efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 250</text><rect x="190" y="138" width="46" height="12" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="134" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 25</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">BOD (mg/L)</text></svg>
figure.caption: BOD: influent vs. effluent (mg/L).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta=\dfrac{S_0-S_e}{S_0}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{250-25}{250}\times100=90\%.$$

**Answer.** $90\%$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the rectangular settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (in MLD) and the plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 8 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 400 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $SOR=\dfrac{Q}{A}$ (1 MLD = 1000 m³/day), with $Q$ and $A$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times1000}{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m/day}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 6</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{6}}=75620.$$

**Answer.** $₹75620$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.1 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.1\times200/6})\times100=96.43\%.$$

**Answer.** $96.43\%$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cradle-to-grave LCA spans extraction → production → use → end-of-life.

**Working.**
Extraction to disposal.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A field device estimates wastewater BOD within minutes by measuring the respiration of immobilised microbes through a dissolved-oxygen electrode. The biological recognition element and the transducer are, respectively,
options: immobilised microorganisms and the dissolved-oxygen electrode
the DO electrode and the microbes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a microbial BOD biosensor the immobilised microbes are the bio-recognition element; the O₂ they consume is sensed by the DO electrode (the transducer).

**Working.**
Recognition = microbes; transducer = DO electrode.

**Answer.** Microbes; DO electrode

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=150\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 40</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times150}{40+150}=0.237.$$

**Answer.** $0.237$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.05\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $8$ yr, $N=$ _____.

**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

$$1000e^{0.05\times8}=1492.$$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=3.75\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $3.75\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In a stream, $k_d=0.2\,\text{d}^{-1}$, $k_r=0.4\,\text{d}^{-1}$, initial deficit $D_0=2\,\text{mg/L}$, initial BOD $L_0=20\,\text{mg/L}$. The critical time of maximum deficit is _____ days.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 200" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="30" x2="34" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="175" x2="330" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="8" y="40" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">DO</text><text x="305" y="190" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">distance/time →</text><line x1="34" y1="45" x2="330" y2="45" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="250" y="42" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">saturation DO</text><path d="M34 60 Q120 175 175 150 Q260 110 330 70" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="175" y1="45" x2="175" y2="150" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="175" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="180" y="120" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">D_c = 2.7 mg/L</text><text x="150" y="170" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">t_c = 2.9 d</text></svg>
figure.caption: Oxygen-sag curve: critical deficit D_c at critical time t_c.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $t_c=\dfrac{1}{k_r-k_d}\ln\!\left[\dfrac{k_r}{k_d}\left(1-\dfrac{D_0(k_r-k_d)}{k_dL_0}\right)\right]$.

**Working.**
$$t_c=\dfrac{1}{0.4-0.2}\ln\!\left[\dfrac{0.4}{0.2}\left(1-\dfrac{2(0.4-0.2)}{0.2\times20}\right)\right]=2.939.$$

**Answer.** $2.939\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 3</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{3}}=95260.$$

**Answer.** $₹95260$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.05\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $11$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 500</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$500e^{0.05\times11}=867.$$

**Answer.** $867$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=150\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 60</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times150}{60+150}=0.214.$$

**Answer.** $0.214$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.2\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $4$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="110" width="60" height="40" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 80</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$80e^{-0.2\times4}=35.95.$$

**Answer.** $35.95$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">2</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">0.5</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$100+2\times28+0.5\times265=288.5.$$

**Answer.** $288.5$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=80\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times6\times30\times15}e^{-50^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00003646.$$

**Answer.** $0.365\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $25\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">25</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times25}{25}=0.05.$$

**Answer.** $0.05\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $3$ identical machines each produce $85\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log_{10}3=89.77.$$

**Answer.** $89.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=45\%$, $H=5\%$, $O=40\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 15.2</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(45)+1419(5-40/8)=15165.$$

**Answer.** $15165\,\text{kJ/kg}$
